rxn_class_summary: Cathodic reduction of an electrochemically generated N-acyliminium 'cation pool' to a carbon-centered α-amido radical, which adds to activated olefins (e.g., methyl acrylate) to forge C–C bonds (formal C–H addition to C=C).
electrochemical_role: Two-stage electrolysis in a divided cell: anodic oxidation of N-(methoxycarbonyl)pyrrolidine at -72 °C generates an acyliminium cation pool; subsequent cathodic one-electron reduction of the acyliminium cation produces an α-amido carbon radical that couples with the olefin, and a second cathodic reduction of the adduct radical gives a carbanion trapped by proton.

key_intermediates.name: α-amido carbon radical (3) from one-electron reduction of 2
α-amido carbanion (5) from two-electron reduction of 2
adduct radical after addition to methyl acrylate (8)
adduct carbanion (9) from further one-electron reduction of 8

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the carbamate by 2e-/-H+ to give an acyliminium cation pool; at the cathode, one-electron reduction yields an α-amido radical that adds to the electron-deficient olefin (path A); the resulting electron-poor adduct radical is reduced by a second electron to a carbanion, which is protonated by added TfOH to give the cross-coupled product.

probe_reagents.name: trifluoromethanesulfonic acid (TfOH) as proton source / carbanion trap
probe_reagents.observation: Increasing TfOH (0→50 equiv) raised yield of cross-coupled 6 from 34% to 88% and suppressed 2:1 adduct 7; yield of reduced 1 did not increase with excess TfOH, ruling out path B (carbanion 5 attacking olefin).

probe_reagents.name: zinc metal (chemical reductant control)
probe_reagents.observation: Chemical 2e- reduction gave reduced product 1 (54%, major) with only 16% homocoupled 4, indicating that under chemical reduction the carbanion pathway dominates, whereas electrochemical reduction favors the radical coupling pathway.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of cation 2
probe_reagents.observation: Acyliminium cation 2 shows reduction wave at -0.85 V vs Ag/AgCl in Bu4NBF4/CH2Cl2, supporting feasibility of cathodic one-electron reduction.

probe_reagents.name: 1H NMR control with TfOH
probe_reagents.observation: TfOH did not appreciably affect iminium cation 2 by 1H NMR, excluding protonation/decomposition of the cation by the proton source.

notes: Two mechanistic pathways were considered: favored path A involves one-electron cathodic reduction of acyliminium 2 to radical 3, radical addition to acrylate, then a second one-electron reduction to carbanion 9, and protonation. Alternative path B (two-electron reduction to carbanion 5 followed by Michael addition) was excluded because excess TfOH did not increase the yield of simple reduction product 1, which would be expected if 5 were a key intermediate. A competing homocoupling of radical 3 produces dimer 4, and oligomerization gives a 2:1 adduct 7; both are minimized at high TfOH (50 equiv) and 5 equiv acrylate.
